questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $f(x)$ is a quadratic function such that $f(0)=1$ and $\int \frac{f(x) d x}{x^2(x+1)^3}$ is a rational function, then the value of $f^{\prime}(0)$ is ______
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & \text { Let } g(x)=\int \frac{f(x) d x}{x^2(x+1)^3} \ldots(1)\\ & =\int\left(\frac{A}{x}+\frac{B}{x^2}+\frac{C}{x+1}+\frac{D}{(x+1)^2}+\frac{E}{(x+1)^3}\right) d x \\ & =A \ln x-\frac{B}{x}+C \ln (1+x)-\frac{D}{1+x}-\frac{E}{2(x+1)^2}\end{aligned}$

Since $g(x)$ is a rational function hence $A=C=0$
$g(x)=\int\left(\frac{B}{x^2}+\frac{D}{(x+1)^2}+\frac{E}{(x+1)^3}\right) d x\ldots(2)$


Comparing (1) and (2), we get $f(x)=(B+D) x^3+(3 B+D+E) x^2+3 B x+B$ $\Rightarrow \mathrm{f}(\mathrm{x})$ is quadratic
$\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow B+D=0, f(0)=1 \text { gives } B=1 \Rightarrow D=-1 \\ & \therefore f(x)=(2+E) x^2+3 x+1 \\ & f^{\prime}(0)=3\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $\int \frac{\operatorname{cosec}^2 x-2019}{(\cos x)^{2019}} d x=\frac{-f(x)}{(g(x))^{2019}}+C$, then find the value of $\left|f\left(\frac{\pi}{4}\right)+g(0)\right|$. (where $C$ is constant of integration.)
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \int \underbrace{\int\left(\frac{1}{(\cos x)^{2019}}\right)}_{\text {I }} \cdot \underbrace{\operatorname{cosec}^2 x}_{\text {II }} d x-2019 \int \frac{d x}{(\cos x)^{2019}} \\ & \frac{1}{(\cos x)^{2019}} \cdot(-\cot x)+2019 \int \frac{\sin x}{(\cos x)^{2020}} \cdot \cot x d x . \\ & \therefore \quad f(x)=\cot x ; \quad g(x)=\cos x \\ & \Rightarrow \quad\left|f\left(\frac{\pi}{4}\right)+g(0)\right|=2\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $\int(\sin 101 \mathrm{x}) \cdot \sin ^{99} \mathrm{xdx}$ is equal to $\frac{\sin (100 \mathrm{x})(\sin x)^{100}}{25 \times \mathrm{K}}+\mathrm{C}$ then K is equal to
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & I=\int \sin (100 x+x) \cdot \sin ^{99} x d x=\int\{(\sin 100 x) \cos x+\cos 100 x \cdot \sin x\} \sin ^{99} x d x \\ & =\int \sin 100 x \cdot \cos x \cdot \sin ^{99} x d x+\int \cos 100 x \cdot \sin ^{100} x d x \\ & =\frac{\sin (100 x)(\sin x)^{100}}{100}-\frac{100}{100} \int \cos (100 x) \cdot(\sin x)^{100} d x+\int \cos 100 x \cdot \sin ^{100} x d x \\ & =\frac{\sin (100 x)(\sin x)^{100}}{100}+C\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $I=\int \frac{\left(e^x-1\right)(\sin x-\cos x)+x \cos x}{\sin ^2 x+\left(e^x-1-x\right)^2} d x=\tan ^{-1}(f(x))+C$, where ' $C$ ' is the constant of integration and $\lim _{x \rightarrow 0} f(x)=0$. If $\lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{f(x)}{x}=\frac{p}{q}$, where $p, q \in N$ then find the least value of $(p+q)$.
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & I=\int \frac{\left(e^x-1\right)(\sin x-\cos x)+x \cos x}{\sin ^2 x\left(1+\left(\frac{e^x-1-x}{\sin x}\right)^2\right)} d x \\ & \text { Put, } \frac{e^x-1-x}{\sin x}=t \Rightarrow \frac{(\sin x)\left(e^x-1\right)-\left(e^x-1-x\right) \cos x}{\sin ^2 x} d x=d t \\ & \Rightarrow \frac{\left(e^x-1\right)(\sin x-\cos x)+x \cos x}{\sin ^2 x} d x=d t \\ & \therefore \quad I=\int \frac{d t}{1+t^2}=\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{e^x-1-x}{\sin x}\right)+C \\ & \therefore \quad f(x)=\frac{e^x-1-x}{\sin x} \\ & \quad \lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{f(x)}{x}=\lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{e^x-1-x}{x\left(\frac{\sin x}{x}\right) x}=\frac{1}{2} \equiv \frac{p}{q} \\ & \left.\therefore(p+q)\right|_{\text {least }}=3\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $f(x)=\frac{1}{8} \int\left(8 \sin ^7 x \cos x+8 \sin ^3 x \cos x+8 \sin x \cos x\right)\left(\sin ^8 x+2 \sin ^4 x+4 \sin ^2 x\right)^{\frac{1}{4}}$ Find $\left(10 \times f\left(\frac{\pi}{2}\right)\right)^{\frac{4}{3}}$, given that $f(0)=0$
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & f(x)=\int\left(\sin ^6 x+\sin ^2 x+1\right)\left(\sin ^{12} x+2 \sin ^8 x+4 \sin ^6 x\right)^{\frac{1}{4}} \cos x d x \\ & \Rightarrow f(x)=\frac{1}{8} \int\left(8 \sin ^7 x \cos x+8 \sin ^2 x \cos x+8 \sin x \cos x\right)\left(\sin ^8 x+2 \sin ^4 x+4 \sin ^2 x\right)^{\frac{1}{4}} \\ & \text { Let } \sin ^8 x+2 \sin ^4 x+4 \sin ^2 x=t^4,\left(8 \sin ^7 x \cos x+8 \sin ^3 x \cos x+8 \sin x \cos x\right) d x=4 t^3 d t \\ & \Rightarrow f(x)=\frac{1}{2} \int t^4 d t \\ & \Rightarrow f(x)=\frac{\left(\sin ^8 x+2 \sin ^4 x+4 \sin ^2 x\right)^{\frac{5}{4}}}{10}+C \\ & \text { Now } f(0)=0 \Rightarrow C=0 \\ & \left(10 x f\left(\frac{\pi}{2}\right)\right)^{\frac{4}{5}}=7\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $\int \frac{x^2}{x^4+3 x^2+9} d x=A \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{x^2-3}{3 x}\right)+\frac{B}{\sqrt{3}} \ln \left|\frac{x^2-\sqrt{3} x+3}{x^2+\sqrt{3} x+3}\right|+c$, then find the value of $12(A+B)$
questions.solution:
$\int \frac{x^2}{x^4+3 x^2+9} d x=\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{x^2+3+x^2-3}{x^4+9+3 x^2} d x$
$=\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{d\left(x-\frac{3}{x}\right)}{\left(x-\frac{3}{x}\right)^2+9}+\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{d\left(x+\frac{3}{x}\right)}{\left(x+\frac{3}{x}\right)^2-3}=\frac{1}{6} \tan ^{-1} \frac{x^2-3}{3 x}+\frac{1}{4 \sqrt{3}} \ln \left|\frac{x^2-\sqrt{3} x+3}{x^2+\sqrt{3} x+3}\right|+c$
$\begin{aligned} & \therefore \quad A=\frac{1}{6}, B=\frac{1}{4} \\ & \therefore \quad 12(A+B)=5\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $f(x)=\int x^{\sin x}(1+x \cos x \ell n x+\sin x) d x$ and $f\left(\frac{\pi}{2}\right)=\frac{\pi^2}{4}$, then the value of $\{\cos \{f(x)\} \mid$ is
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & f(x)=\int x^{\sin x}(1+x \cos x \ln x+\sin x) d x \\ & \text { Let } f(x)=x^{\sin x} \\ & \frac{1}{f(x)} \cdot f^{\prime}(x)=\sin x \cdot \frac{1}{x}+\ln x \cos x \text { (Taking log on both side and differentiating) } \\ & f^{\prime}(x)=f(x)\left[\frac{\sin x+x \ln x \cos x}{x}\right] \\ & x f^{\prime}(x)=f(x)\left[\sin x+x \cos x x f^{\prime}(x)=f(x)[\sin x+x \cos x \cdot \ln x]\right. \\ & \therefore f(x)=\int[f(x)+f(x)(x \cos x \ell n x+\sin x)] d x \\ & =\int\left[f(x)+x f^{\prime}(x)\right] d x=\int \frac{d}{d x}\{x f(x)\}=x f(x)+c \\ & \therefore f(x)=x x^{\sin x}+c \Rightarrow f\left(\frac{\pi}{2}\right)=\frac{\pi}{2} \cdot \frac{\pi^{\prime}}{2}+c=\frac{\pi^2}{4} \Rightarrow c=0 \\ & \therefore f|\cos \{f(\pi)\}|=f|\cos (\pi)|=f(1)=1\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
